metadata.summary: Production-ready Terraform HCL modules for GCP: VPC, firewall rules, service account (least privilege), Cloud Run v2, GKE Autopilot, Workload Identity, and Cloud SQL with Secret Manager integration.

content.title: GCP Terraform Templates
content.summary: Production-ready Terraform HCL module set for GCP infrastructure (2025-2026): VPC with four subnet tiers and Cloud NAT, least-privilege firewall rules, per-workload service accounts, Cloud Run v2 service with probes, GKE Autopilot with Workload Identity, Cloud SQL PostgreSQL with private IP and PITR, and a complete example composing all modules.

why.p: Terraform modules encapsulate GCP best practices (private IPs, Shielded VMs, CMEK, PITR) into reusable units. Without modules, teams hand-copy gcloud commands and miss critical flags (deletion_protection, enable_private_endpoint, workload_pool). Module composition from a root main.tf keeps environment-specific config minimal and auditable.

list.item: Bootstrapping a new GCP project with production-ready infrastructure via Terraform.
Adding a Cloud Run service, GKE cluster, or Cloud SQL instance to an existing Terraform root.
Standardizing service account and IAM patterns across teams via shared modules.
Generating LLM-assisted Terraform for GCP (use gcp-llm-prompts for prompt templates).
One-off exploratory deployments — use gcloud commands (gcp-overview-cli or gcp-compute-gke) and destroy after.
Existing infrastructure managed outside Terraform — import first, then adopt modules incrementally.

example.title: modules/vpc/main.tf — VPC with four subnets and Cloud NAT
modules/firewall/main.tf — least-privilege firewall rules
modules/service-account/main.tf — least-privilege SA
modules/cloud-run/main.tf — Cloud Run v2 service with probes
modules/gke-autopilot/main.tf — private Autopilot cluster
modules/workload-identity/main.tf — GKE pod WI binding
modules/cloud-sql/main.tf — PostgreSQL with private IP and PITR
main.tf — compose all modules into a complete environment

rule.statement: Set deletion_protection = true on Cloud SQL instances and GKE clusters in production. Use provider version constraints (~> 5.0) and terraform required_version (>= 1.5). Store state in GCS backend with versioning and locking.
rule.rationale: deletion_protection prevents accidental terraform destroy from dropping stateful resources. Version constraints ensure reproducibility across environments and CI runs.
